subtopics.title: Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD)
subtopics.definition: Optimizer that updates weights using random samples
subtopics.explanation: SGD updates model parameters using a single random sample or mini-batch at each iteration, rather than the full dataset. This introduces noise that helps escape local minima and enables training on large datasets. Standard SGD: θ = θ - η·∇J(θ). Variants: Mini-batch SGD (most common, batch size 16-512), SGD with Momentum (adds inertia to accelerate convergence). Advantages: memory efficient, faster updates, escapes shallow local minima. Disadvantages: noisy updates, requires careful learning rate tuning, can oscillate in narrow valleys.
subtopics.example: For 1M samples, batch GD computes gradients on all 1M (slow, one update). SGD with batch=32 computes gradients on 32 samples (fast, 31,250 updates per epoch). Noise helps escape local minima.
subtopics.code:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.optim as optim
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

class SGDImplementation:
    """Manual SGD implementation"""
    
    def __init__(self, learning_rate=0.01):
        self.lr = learning_rate
    
    def update(self, params, grads):
        """Update parameters using gradients"""
        for param, grad in zip(params, grads):
            param -= self.lr * grad

class SGDWithMomentum:
    """SGD with momentum"""
    
    def __init__(self, learning_rate=0.01, momentum=0.9):
        self.lr = learning_rate
        self.momentum = momentum
        self.velocities = {}
    
    def update(self, params, grads):
        for i, (param, grad) in enumerate(zip(params, grads)):
            if i not in self.velocities:
                self.velocities[i] = torch.zeros_like(param)
            
            self.velocities[i] = self.momentum * self.velocities[i] + self.lr * grad
            param -= self.velocities[i]

class NesterovSGD:
    """Nesterov Accelerated Gradient (NAG)"""
    
    def __init__(self, learning_rate=0.01, momentum=0.9):
        self.lr = learning_rate
        self.momentum = momentum
        self.velocities = {}
    
    def update(self, params, grads):
        for i, (param, grad) in enumerate(zip(params, grads)):
            if i not in self.velocities:
                self.velocities[i] = torch.zeros_like(param)
            
            # Look ahead
            lookahead = param - self.momentum * self.velocities[i]
            # Gradient at lookahead (simplified)
            self.velocities[i] = self.momentum * self.velocities[i] + self.lr * grad
            param -= self.velocities[i]

# Demonstration
print("🔷 Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD)")
print("=" * 60)

# Create simple model
model = nn.Linear(10, 1)
criterion = nn.MSELoss()

# Different SGD variants
optimizers = {
    'SGD': optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01),
    'SGD Momentum': optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01, momentum=0.9),
    'SGD Nesterov': optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01, momentum=0.9, nesterov=True),
    'SGD Weight Decay': optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01, weight_decay=1e-4)
}

print("\n📊 SGD Variants:")
for name in optimizers.keys():
    print(f"  • {name}")

print("\n📊 SGD Parameters:")
print("  • learning_rate (lr): Step size (0.001-0.1 typical)")
print("  • momentum: Acceleration factor (0.9 typical)")
print("  • weight_decay: L2 regularization (1e-4 typical)")
print("  • nesterov: Use Nesterov accelerated gradient")

print("\n✅ SGD Characteristics:")
print("  • Advantages: Simple, memory efficient, escapes local minima")
print("  • Disadvantages: Noisy, sensitive to learning rate, oscillations")
print("  • Best for: Large datasets, convex problems, when memory is limited")
print("\n💡 Learning Rate Guidelines:")
print("  • Too high: Divergence, loss increases")
print("  • Too low: Slow convergence, may get stuck")
print("  • Just right: Steady decrease in loss")
print("  • Typical range: 0.1 → 0.01 → 0.001 → 0.0001")

subtopics.title: Adam Optimizer
subtopics.explanation: Adam combines advantages of SGD with momentum (first moment) and RMSProp (second moment) with adaptive learning rates. Computes: m_t = β₁·m_{t-1} + (1-β₁)·g_t (mean), v_t = β₂·v_{t-1} + (1-β₂)·g_t² (uncentered variance), m̂_t = m_t/(1-β₁ᵗ), v̂_t = v_t/(1-β₂ᵗ), θ_t = θ_{t-1} - α·m̂_t/(√v̂_t+ε). Defaults: α=0.001, β₁=0.9, β₂=0.999, ε=1e-8. Benefits: works well for most problems, less sensitive to learning rate, handles sparse gradients, converges faster. Disadvantages: can generalize worse than SGD for some tasks (image classification).
subtopics.example: Sparse gradients (NLP): Adam adapts learning rates per parameter. Features that appear rarely get larger updates. Training converges 2-3x faster than SGD on many tasks.
subtopics.code:
class AdamOptimizer:
    """Manual Adam implementation"""
    
    def __init__(self, params, lr=0.001, betas=(0.9, 0.999), eps=1e-8, weight_decay=0):
        self.params = list(params)
        self.lr = lr
        self.beta1, self.beta2 = betas
        self.eps = eps
        self.weight_decay = weight_decay
        self.t = 0
        self.m = [torch.zeros_like(p) for p in self.params]
        self.v = [torch.zeros_like(p) for p in self.params]
    
    def step(self, grads):
        self.t += 1
        
        for i, (param, grad) in enumerate(zip(self.params, grads)):
            if self.weight_decay > 0:
                grad = grad + self.weight_decay * param
            
            # Update biased first moment estimate
            self.m[i] = self.beta1 * self.m[i] + (1 - self.beta1) * grad
            
            # Update biased second raw moment estimate
            self.v[i] = self.beta2 * self.v[i] + (1 - self.beta2) * (grad ** 2)
            
            # Compute bias-corrected estimates
            m_hat = self.m[i] / (1 - self.beta1 ** self.t)
            v_hat = self.v[i] / (1 - self.beta2 ** self.t)
            
            # Update parameters
            param -= self.lr * m_hat / (torch.sqrt(v_hat) + self.eps)
    
    def zero_grad(self):
        for param in self.params:
            if param.grad is not None:
                param.grad.zero_()

class AdamWOptimizer(AdamOptimizer):
    """Adam with decoupled weight decay (better generalization)"""
    
    def step(self, grads):
        self.t += 1
        
        for i, (param, grad) in enumerate(zip(self.params, grads)):
            # Update biased first moment estimate
            self.m[i] = self.beta1 * self.m[i] + (1 - self.beta1) * grad
            
            # Update biased second raw moment estimate
            self.v[i] = self.beta2 * self.v[i] + (1 - self.beta2) * (grad ** 2)
            
            # Compute bias-corrected estimates
            m_hat = self.m[i] / (1 - self.beta1 ** self.t)
            v_hat = self.v[i] / (1 - self.beta2 ** self.t)
            
            # Update parameters with decoupled weight decay
            param -= self.lr * (m_hat / (torch.sqrt(v_hat) + self.eps) + self.weight_decay * param)

# PyTorch Adam variants
print("\n🔷 Adam Optimizer")
print("=" * 60)

model = nn.Linear(10, 1)
adam_optimizers = {
    'Adam': optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=0.001),
    'AdamW': optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=0.001, weight_decay=0.01),
    'Adamax': optim.Adamax(model.parameters(), lr=0.002),
    'NAdam': optim.NAdam(model.parameters(), lr=0.001)
}

print("\n📊 Adam Variants:")
for name in adam_optimizers.keys():
    print(f"  • {name}")

print("\n📊 Adam Parameters:")
print("  • lr: Learning rate (default: 0.001)")
print("  • betas: (β₁, β₂) for moment estimates (default: 0.9, 0.999)")
print("  • eps: Numerical stability (default: 1e-8)")
print("  • weight_decay: L2 regularization (default: 0)")

print("\n✅ Adam Advantages:")
print("  • Adaptive learning rates per parameter")
print("  • Works well out-of-box (less tuning)")
print("  • Handles sparse gradients well")
print("  • Fast convergence")
print("  • Momentum + RMSProp benefits")
print("\n⚠️ Adam Disadvantages:")
print("  • May generalize worse than SGD")
print("  • More memory usage (stores m and v)")
print("  • Can overfit without proper regularization")
print("\n💡 When to Use:")
print("  • Adam: NLP, transformers, GANs, most tasks")
print("  • AdamW: When weight decay is important (better generalization)")
print("  • SGD: Image classification, when you need best test accuracy")

subtopics.title: Learning Rate Scheduling
subtopics.definition: Dynamically adjusting learning rate during training
subtopics.explanation: Learning rate scheduling reduces learning rate over time to improve convergence. Strategies: 1) Step decay: reduce by factor every N epochs, 2) Exponential decay: lr = lr₀·γᵉᵖᵒᶜʰ, 3) Cosine annealing: cyclic cosine schedule, 4) Reduce on plateau: reduce when metric stops improving, 5) Warmup: gradual increase initially. Benefits: better convergence, avoids oscillations, fine-tunes at end. Modern schedulers: OneCycle (increases then decreases), Cosine with restarts (periodic increases to escape local minima).
subtopics.example: Start lr=0.1, step decay (γ=0.1 every 30 epochs): epochs 0-29: 0.1, 30-59: 0.01, 60-89: 0.001. Allows fast initial progress then fine-tuning.
subtopics.code:
import math

class LearningRateSchedulers:
    """Various learning rate scheduling strategies"""
    
    def __init__(self, optimizer, initial_lr=0.1):
        self.optimizer = optimizer
        self.initial_lr = initial_lr
        self.base_lr = initial_lr
    
    def step_decay(self, epoch, drop_rate=0.5, epochs_drop=10):
        """Step decay: reduce by factor every N epochs"""
        lr = self.base_lr * (drop_rate ** (epoch // epochs_drop))
        for param_group in self.optimizer.param_groups:
            param_group['lr'] = lr
        return lr
    
    def exponential_decay(self, epoch, gamma=0.95):
        """Exponential decay: lr = lr₀·γᵉᵖᵒᶜʰ"""
        lr = self.base_lr * (gamma ** epoch)
        for param_group in self.optimizer.param_groups:
            param_group['lr'] = lr
        return lr
    
    def cosine_annealing(self, epoch, T_max=100, eta_min=0):
        """Cosine annealing: lr = η_min + (η_max - η_min)·(1 + cos(π·epoch/T_max))/2"""
        lr = eta_min + (self.base_lr - eta_min) * (1 + math.cos(math.pi * epoch / T_max)) / 2
        for param_group in self.optimizer.param_groups:
            param_group['lr'] = lr
        return lr
    
    def reduce_on_plateau(self, metric, patience=10, factor=0.5, min_lr=1e-6):
        """Reduce lr when metric stops improving"""
        if not hasattr(self, 'best_metric'):
            self.best_metric = metric
            self.patience_counter = 0
            return
        
        if metric < self.best_metric:
            self.best_metric = metric
            self.patience_counter = 0
        else:
            self.patience_counter += 1
            if self.patience_counter >= patience:
                current_lr = self.optimizer.param_groups[0]['lr']
                new_lr = max(current_lr * factor, min_lr)
                for param_group in self.optimizer.param_groups:
                    param_group['lr'] = new_lr
                self.patience_counter = 0
    
    def cyclic_lr(self, epoch, cycle_length=10, max_lr=None, min_lr=0.0001):
        """Cyclic learning rate (oscillates between bounds)"""
        if max_lr is None:
            max_lr = self.base_lr
        
        cycle = epoch // cycle_length
        cycle_epoch = epoch % cycle_length
        
        # Linear interpolation within cycle
        lr = min_lr + (max_lr - min_lr) * (cycle_epoch / cycle_length)
        
        for param_group in self.optimizer.param_groups:
            param_group['lr'] = lr
        return lr
    
    def one_cycle(self, epoch, total_epochs, max_lr=0.1, min_lr=0.0001):
        """One-cycle policy: increase then decrease"""
        if epoch < total_epochs / 2:
            # Increasing phase
            progress = epoch / (total_epochs / 2)
            lr = min_lr + (max_lr - min_lr) * progress
        else:
            # Decreasing phase
            progress = (epoch - total_epochs / 2) / (total_epochs / 2)
            lr = max_lr - (max_lr - min_lr) * progress
        
        for param_group in self.optimizer.param_groups:
            param_group['lr'] = lr
        return lr

# PyTorch schedulers
print("\n🔷 Learning Rate Scheduling")
print("=" * 60)

model = nn.Linear(10, 1)
optimizer = optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1)

schedulers = {
    'StepLR': optim.lr_scheduler.StepLR(optimizer, step_size=30, gamma=0.1),
    'ExponentialLR': optim.lr_scheduler.ExponentialLR(optimizer, gamma=0.95),
    'CosineAnnealingLR': optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=100),
    'ReduceLROnPlateau': optim.lr_scheduler.ReduceLROnPlateau(optimizer, patience=10, factor=0.5),
    'CyclicLR': optim.lr_scheduler.CyclicLR(optimizer, base_lr=0.001, max_lr=0.1),
    'OneCycleLR': optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(optimizer, max_lr=0.1, total_steps=100)
}

print("\n📊 Available Schedulers:")
for name in schedulers.keys():
    print(f"  • {name}")

# Simulate learning rate schedules
print("\n📊 Learning Rate Schedules (100 epochs):")
print("-" * 60)

scheduler_demo = LearningRateSchedulers(optimizer, initial_lr=0.1)

print("\nStep Decay (drop every 30 epochs):")
lrs = []
for epoch in range(100):
    lr = scheduler_demo.step_decay(epoch, drop_rate=0.1, epochs_drop=30)
    if epoch in [0, 29, 30, 59, 60, 89]:
        print(f"  Epoch {epoch}: lr = {lr:.6f}")

print("\nCosine Annealing (T_max=100):")
scheduler_demo.base_lr = 0.1
for epoch in range(0, 101, 20):
    lr = scheduler_demo.cosine_annealing(epoch, T_max=100, eta_min=0)
    print(f"  Epoch {epoch}: lr = {lr:.6f}")

print("\nOne-Cycle (total=100 epochs):")
scheduler_demo.base_lr = 0.01
for epoch in [0, 25, 50, 75, 100]:
    lr = scheduler_demo.one_cycle(epoch, total_epochs=100, max_lr=0.1, min_lr=0.0001)
    print(f"  Epoch {epoch}: lr = {lr:.6f}")

print("\n✅ Scheduling Strategies:")
print("  • Step Decay: Simple, effective, reduces at fixed intervals")
print("  • Exponential: Smooth decay, good for long training")
print("  • Cosine Annealing: Warm restarts, escapes local minima")
print("  • Reduce on Plateau: Adaptive, reduces when stuck")
print("  • One-Cycle: Increase then decrease, fast convergence")
print("  • Warmup: Gradual start, prevents early divergence")

print("\n💡 Scheduling Best Practices:")
print("  • Start with constant lr, add step decay if loss plateaus")
print("  • Use cosine annealing for long training (>100 epochs)")
print("  • One-cycle for fast convergence (1-10 epochs)")
print("  • Reduce on plateau for adaptive tuning")
print("  • Warmup for transformers and large batch sizes")
print("\n📊 Optimizer + Scheduler Combinations:")
print("  • Adam + CosineAnnealing: Good for transformers")
print("  • SGD + StepLR: Classic for image classification")
print("  • AdamW + CosineAnnealing: Modern best practice")
print("  • SGD + ReduceOnPlateau: Adaptive fine-tuning")
print("  • Any + OneCycle: Fast convergence")
